page: Amazon Web Services c8in.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance featuring dedicated Intel processors. The hardware configuration delivers 384 vCPUs across 192 physical cores, utilizing an x86_64 architecture with 2.0 threads per core. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This low memory-to-core ratio is characteristic of compute-focused hardware profiles. Notably, the server does not include any local storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on external network-attached storage volumes.

Network performance is a key feature of this instance, which provides a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. It does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is designed for large-scale compute-bound workloads, network-intensive applications, distributed analytics, and high-throughput batch processing where local storage is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services c8in.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel processors running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 384 vCPUs across 192 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The system is configured with 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage included, requiring reliance on network-attached storage. However, the instance offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps to support data-intensive transfers. Lacking integrated GPUs, this server is designed for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads, network-intensive applications, and large-scale distributed processing tasks that demand high-speed networking and dedicated CPU resources without the need for local physical disks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 384 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for highly parallelized, network-heavy workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized instance featuring three hundred and eighty-four vCPUs and three hundred gigabits per second baseline network bandwidth.
